article_meta.title: Tillis: 'Noem Is Out of Her Depth,' She's 'Not Competent' 7 Pam Key28 Jan 2026 Wednesday on CNN's "The Lead," Sen

transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective included

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: Tillis: 'Noem Is Out of Her Depth,' She's 'Not Competent' 7 Pam Key28 Jan 2026 Wednesday on CNN's "The Lead," Sen
SOURCE: Breitbart Fact Check

ARTICLE:

Tillis: 'Noem Is Out of Her Depth,' She's 'Not Competent' 7 Pam Key28 Jan 2026 Wednesday on CNN's "The Lead," Sen. Thom Tillis (R-NC) said Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem "is not competent" to run the federal agency. Tillis said, "Well, I think they're doing the American people and the president a great disservice by not realizing how much attention is focused on these operations and how details and discipline matter. They spoke before they knew anything about what occurred on the ground. They didn't even implement the typical protocol that you would. And an officer involved shooting as much to protect those officers as the scene of the incident." He added, "Everything that goes into an investigation after a casualty, after a death. They talked too soon. It looked like they wanted to get ahead of a news cycle. And it really discredits it. It brings their credibility into question. And, Jake, it's very important for people in North Carolina to know I've held every sheriff in North Carolina that was a sanctuary city, jurisdiction accountable. We went after them. Our office filed a bill for justice for sanctuary, a justice for victims of sanctuary cities, to make sure that if somebody was harmed by someone who was detained and not transferred to ICE, that they could sue that local government and get restitution. So I've made it very clear that I want law enforcement to work with each other, but you can't be situational. You can't expect that out of a Biden administration and Democrat mayors, and then not expect us to lead by example by coordinating with state and local law enforcement. I believe that Noem is out of her depth. I think she's reached the Peter Principle. She is not competent to run this organization, and I haven't even begun to talk about the failure of FEMA and disaster response in North Carolina, but I will be tomorrow on the floor." Follow Pam Key on X @pamkeyNEN ClipsPoliticsDonald TrumpKristi NoemStephen MillerThom Tillis
---

{
  "summary": "In a recent interview on CNN's 'The Lead,' Senator Thom Tillis (R-NC) harshly criticized Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem, stating she is 'not competent' and 'out of her depth' in managing the federal agency. Tillis argued that Noem and her team mishandled key protocols following a recent officer-involved shooting, speaking publicly before gathering necessary information and not adhering to standard investigation practices. He claimed this behavior undermined their credibility and was an attempt to control the news narrative prematurely. Tillis contrasted this with his own record in North Carolina, where he has taken action against sanctuary city policies and supported measures to hold local jurisdictions accountable when cooperation with ICE was neglected. He emphasized the importance of consistent coordination between federal, state, and local law enforcement, criticizing both the Biden administration and Democratic mayors for what he sees as situational enforcement. Tillis concluded that Noem’s performance exhibits the Peter Principle, suggesting she has risen above her level of competence, and indicated further criticism of FEMA's disaster response would follow.",
  "key_facts": [
    "Sen. Thom Tillis publicly criticized Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem’s competence on CNN.",
    "Tillis accused Noem and her team of speaking prematurely and mishandling protocols after an officer-involved shooting.",
    "He asserted Noem's actions damaged the agency's credibility and appeared aimed at news cycle management.",
    "Tillis highlighted his own efforts in North Carolina to hold sanctuary cities accountable for cooperating with ICE.",
    "Senator Tillis promised further critique of Noem’s handling of FEMA and disaster response in North Carolina."
  ],
  "sources_represented": ["Breitbart"]
}

parsed_output.summary: In a recent interview on CNN's 'The Lead,' Senator Thom Tillis (R-NC) harshly criticized Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem, stating she is 'not competent' and 'out of her depth' in managing the federal agency. Tillis argued that Noem and her team mishandled key protocols following a recent officer-involved shooting, speaking publicly before gathering necessary information and not adhering to standard investigation practices. He claimed this behavior undermined their credibility and was an attempt to control the news narrative prematurely. Tillis contrasted this with his own record in North Carolina, where he has taken action against sanctuary city policies and supported measures to hold local jurisdictions accountable when cooperation with ICE was neglected. He emphasized the importance of consistent coordination between federal, state, and local law enforcement, criticizing both the Biden administration and Democratic mayors for what he sees as situational enforcement. Tillis concluded that Noem’s performance exhibits the Peter Principle, suggesting she has risen above her level of competence, and indicated further criticism of FEMA's disaster response would follow.
key_facts: Sen. Thom Tillis publicly criticized Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem’s competence on CNN.
Tillis accused Noem and her team of speaking prematurely and mishandling protocols after an officer-involved shooting.
He asserted Noem's actions damaged the agency's credibility and appeared aimed at news cycle management.
Tillis highlighted his own efforts in North Carolina to hold sanctuary cities accountable for cooperating with ICE.
Senator Tillis promised further critique of Noem’s handling of FEMA and disaster response in North Carolina.
